headline: Highgate School Opens Its First International Campus in Thailand This August
dek: The 460-year-old North London independent school will welcome its founding cohort in Chonburi in August 2026, marking a significant first for one of Britain's most celebrated day schools.
author: marcus-chen
heroAlt: Highgate School Opens Its First International Campus in Thailand This August

body.text: Highgate International School Thailand is now just months from opening, with enrolment under way for Pre-Prep and Junior School places for children aged two to eleven. The school — Highgate's first international campus anywhere in the world — is being built near Siam Country Club in Chonburi province, less than eighty minutes south-east of Bangkok, in partnership with Thailand's Siam Motors Group and the Phornprapha family.

The campus has been designed to accommodate up to 1,400 day and boarding pupils aged two to eighteen once fully built out. Phase one opens in August 2026 as a co-educational Pre-Prep and Junior School; the Senior School, together with full boarding facilities, is planned to follow in 2027. Pupils will follow the English National Curriculum, progressing to IGCSEs and A Levels.
A landmark first for the Highgate brand

Highgate School was named Independent School of the Decade by The Sunday Times in 2021 and is consistently placed among London's top ten independent schools for academic performance. That the school has chosen Thailand — rather than the more established Hong Kong or Singapore markets — for its global debut reflects both the strength of the Siam Motors partnership and the sustained appetite for British curriculum education across Southeast Asia.

The founding head is Ben Keeling, who spent eight years as Principal of Shrewsbury International School Hong Kong before taking the Chonburi role. His appointment, confirmed earlier this year, was welcomed by Highgate's head Adam Pettitt, who described Keeling's "transformational leadership record" as ideally suited to building a new school from scratch. Teachers are being recruited predominantly from the UK, with the UK campus involved directly in the selection process to ensure alignment with Highgate's ethos.

The purpose-built campus will include an Olympic-sized swimming pool, a FIFA-standard football pitch, a golf academy, a 450-seat auditorium, a black-box theatre, a STEM building, and a library and digital media centre. Those are specifications that would be notable even for an established school; for a founding-year campus, they mark an unusually substantial infrastructure commitment.

Highgate's arrival adds to what is becoming an exceptionally competitive year for British curriculum schools in Thailand. Dulwich College Bangkok and Wycombe Abbey International School Bangkok are also scheduled to open in August 2026, giving families relocating to the country three new premium British options simultaneously. Whether the market can absorb all of them at once — and what that means for established players like Harrow Bangkok and Shrewsbury — will be one of the defining questions of the 2026–27 admissions cycle.
